# Local Docs Viewer
A secure, fully client-side web application for viewing local documentation with a custom "Kindle Oasis" matte dark theme.

## Core Information
- Name: Local Docs Viewer
- Domain: md.fauzanfebrian.my.id
- Author: Fauzan Febrian
- Author Website: fauzanfebrian.my.id
- GitHub Repository: https://github.com/fauzanfebrian/local-docs-viewer
- License: MIT

## Features
- Zero-Server Processing: All parsing, rendering, and logic happen entirely in the browser.
- Kindle Oasis Theme: Matte dark theme optimized for long reading sessions.
- Persistent Workspaces: Uses IndexedDB to remember folder handles across sessions.
- Markdown Support: GitHub Flavored Markdown, math notation, and Mermaid diagrams.
- Native PDF Viewer: Memory-efficient PDF viewing using browser primitives.
- Security: Strict CSP, HTML sanitization via DOMPurify, and zero telemetry.

## Technology Stack
- Framework: React 19 + Vite
- Language: TypeScript
- Routing: React Router 7
- Markdown Rendering: react-markdown, shiki, mermaid
- Storage: idb-keyval (IndexedDB)
- Styling: Vanilla CSS

## Purpose
This tool serves as an online markdown viewer and previewer that runs entirely locally, allowing users to safely view `.md`, `.txt`, and `.pdf` files without uploading them to any external server.
